id: sep24-1
name: Amateurs Fix — 3-Topic List Hook
technique: list
platforms: [linkedin]
tags: [mobile-optimized, listicle, strong-hook, punchy-lines, actionable]
source_month: "2024-09"
---

## Why It Works

It starts by using strong words like "amateurs" in the opening hook and then promising a simple fix with 3 things. The rest of the post is easy to read, mobile-optimized (short, punchy lines) and very digestible.

## Template

Most people [TOPIC] like amateurs.
Fix it by doing these 3 things:
1. [TOPIC 1]:
- [Advice #1]
- [Advice #2]
- [Advice #3]
1. [TOPIC 2]:
- [Advice #4]
- [Advice #5]
- [Advice #6]
1. [TOPIC 3]:
- [Advice #7]
- [Advice #8]
- [Advice #9]
Most people mess these up.
[Common mistake]
Wrong.
[Key principle] is about [Main focus].
If you're struggling to [Challenge] & grow your [Desired outcome], follow my blueprint to [Specific achievement]: [Your link here]
Then, go build that [TOPIC] muscle.

## Example

Most people are amateurs at website optimization.
Fix it by doing these 3 things:
1. On-Page Optimization:
- Craft compelling meta titles and descriptions
- Use header tags (H1, H2, H3) strategically
- Optimize images with alt text and descriptive file names
1. Content Strategy:
- Create high-quality, relevant content regularly
- Target long-tail keywords for better ranking opportunities
- Implement internal linking to boost page authority
1. Technical SEO:
- Improve site speed and mobile responsiveness
- Fix crawl errors and broken links
- Implement schema markup for rich snippets
Most people skip these.
They focus solely on keyword stuffing and ignore user experience.
Wrong.
Effective SEO is about providing value to users while making your site search engine friendly.
If you're struggling to rank higher & grow your organic traffic, follow my blueprint to double your search visibility in 6 months: [Your link here]
Then, go build that SEO traffic!
